text:
“… officials in Abu Dhabi needed enough ships to make the risky transit — not just once, but over and over. 

And for that they turned for help to Ga-Hyun Chung, the intensely private Korean shipping tycoon…”

https://t.co/uCaWia5WvQ

A flotilla of Japanese oil tankers and other vessels is leaving at once the Strait of Hormuz via the Iranian shipping lane. 

The group includes six VLCCs carrying ~12 million barrels from Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates that were trapped for ~100 days.
@trollmasta101 @HormuzLetter Check the time stamp of my tweet. And then think who's doing the word by word copy.

Saudi Arabia slashes the official selling price of its flagship Arab Light, pricing the barrels for Asia delivery at **minus** $1.5 a barrel to the Dubai benchmark.

It's the largest **discount** for Arab Light to Asia since mid-2020, when the world was still battling Covid-19.

CHART OF THE DAY: Saudi Arabia has only offered its flagship Arab Light for Far East delivery at minus $1.5 a barrel (or cheaper) twice in the last 25 years: once during the 2015 price war against US shale, and again in 2020 during the price war against Russia. That's it. https://t.co/LvTeYoPSBP

Cuba suffers another nation-wide power blackout (the 2nd this year to affect the whole of the island). 

Cuba has been suffering from rolling blackouts since mid 2025, at times affecting as much as 75% of the population. But complete, nationwide grid collapses had been rarer.
